nameMeanings.name: Wayte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Waite
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name for a watchman, either in a town or castle, from Anglo-Norman French waite ‘watchman, member of the watch’ (of ancient Germanic origin; compare Wachter ). In the Middle Ages the town waits typically combined the functions of watchmen and musicians through being trumpeters or pipers. There may also have been some late confusion with White or Wheat .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Wayte was born in 1435, in North Bradley, Wiltshire, England, United Kingdom, her father, Edward Wayte, was 25 and her mother, Margaret Bisaillon Wayte Popham, was 21. She had at least 6 sons and 2 daughters with Sir John Longe of Draycot-Cerne, Knight of the Bath. She died on 1 February 1461, in Barton on Sea, Hampshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 26.

Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles avec Sir John Longe of Draycot-Cerne, Knight of the Bath. Elle est décédée le 1 février 1461, à Barton on Sea, Hampshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 26 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Wayte(이)가 1435년 영국 잉글랜드 윌츠히르 노르스 브래들리에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Edward Wayte(은)는 25세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Bisaillon Wayte Popham(은)는 21세였다. 그녀는 Sir John Longe of Draycot-Cerne, Knight of the Bath(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1461년 2월 1일 영국 잉글랜드 함프실르 바턴  시에서 26세의 나이로 사망했다.
